name: Shipping Container Cafe
description:
A shipping container cafe is the iconic version of the format — the corrugated steel silhouette your customers will recognise from a hundred Instagram shots. SAMAN ships three standard configurations: a 20-ft single for a coffee window, a 40-ft single for fast-casual seating, and a 25×20 multi-unit showcase at ₹28,50,000 for a full cafe with kitchen and dine-in. This page is the size-and-cost decision page — if you have already chosen the shipping-container form factor, here is where you pick the configuration.

What "Shipping Container" Means in a SAMAN Build

The shipping-container look most cafe buyers want is the corrugated steel exterior and the boxy ISO silhouette. What most buyers do not ask is whether they are getting a real used freight container or a steel structure built to look like one.

Every SAMAN shipping container cafe is new fabrication built to ISO shipping-container spec — not a refurbished freight container that has spent 15 years carrying chemicals across the ocean. You get the same external silhouette without the food-safety, structural, or paint-history concerns of a used cargo unit. The exterior comes in two finishes: corten-look corrugated steel for the iconic visible-container appearance, or pre-painted GI cladding in your brand colour.

Two finish options for a SAMAN shipping container cafe: corten-look corrugated steel (foreground) and pre-painted GI cladding (background). Both options preserve the ISO shipping-container silhouette.

Inside the wall: 50mm PUF insulation runs through the full cabin shell. That handles the 38–42°C Bangalore summer load with a sealed roof above it for monsoon. Both finish options give you the recognisable shipping-container exterior — the difference is between visible corrugated steel and a clean painted surface for branding.

Size Grid: 20-ft, 40-ft, and the 25×20 Multi-Unit Showcase

Three standard configurations cover most cafe concepts. Match the size to your menu and expected seating, not the other way around.

Configuration Footprint Seats / throughput Starting price
Single 20-ft ~160 sq ft (20×8 ft) Up to 8–10 covers (stool seating or compact dine-in); 30–60 customers/hour From ₹8,50,000
Single 40-ft ~320 sq ft (40×8 ft) 16–20 dine-in seats + counter; 50–90 customers/hour Mid-range — quote on request
25×20 multi-unit (showcase) ~500 sq ft 20–30 dine-in seats + full kitchen + counter ₹28,50,000

The three standard SAMAN shipping container cafe configurations at the same scale — 20-ft single (160 sq ft), 40-ft single (320 sq ft), and the 25×20 multi-unit showcase (500 sq ft).

A 20-ft unit fits a coffee window, a counter, and a small seating area — works for a single-product brand at a campus, mall entrance, or highway stop. A 40-ft unit doubles the floor area and supports a real kitchen behind a service counter with dine-in seating. The 25×20 multi-unit is two 20-ft containers joined and sealed, giving you 500 sq ft total — kitchen at one end, counter in the middle, dine-in seating at the other. This is the SAMAN showcase configuration at ₹28,50,000, currently available for delivery from our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities.

The ₹28,50,000 Showcase — What It Includes and What It Does Not

The 25×20 multi-unit at ₹28,50,000 arrives factory-fitted, not as a bare shell. The price includes the two-container joined structure with corrugated cladding and 50mm PUF insulation, the factory-fitted kitchen zone with exhaust hood position and LPG inlet, food-grade stainless steel lining on the cooking wall, an MCB panel with separate circuits for cooking, lighting, and AC provision, doors and windows per your approved layout, and the exterior finish ready for branding application.

The 25×20 multi-unit shipping container cafe at ₹28,50,000 — two 20-ft container shells joined and sealed into a single 500 sq ft cafe with kitchen, counter, and dine-in seating.

What the price does not include: commercial burners and refrigeration (owner buys per menu), furniture and seating, signage, and FSSAI registration. The full kitchen fit-out specification — exhaust sizing, food-grade surfaces, FSSAI airflow requirements — is documented on our broader container cafe page, which goes into the kitchen build in detail.

If you see ₹2 lakh to ₹3 lakh "container cafe" listings on marketplaces, you are looking at a bare steel shell, not a unit you can open a business in.

When a Shipping Container Cafe Is the Right Pick

A shipping container cafe is right for one specific situation: you want the visible ISO 20-ft, 40-ft, or 25×20 container silhouette as a fixed-location cafe. If your concept needs something else, SAMAN has a dedicated page for it.

If you want… The right page is
The iconic visible-container silhouette as a standalone cafe (20-ft / 40-ft / 25×0) This page
To browse every cafe format we offer — kiosk to multi-unit to mobile to hotel Our complete cafe range
A compact 10-ft setup for coffee or single-product only, with a walk-up window Our compact coffee setup
A larger cafe built from three or more connected container units Our multi-container layout
Full restaurant-style dining service with table service and a larger menu Our full dining-service configuration
A unit that travels between locations on a trailer chassis Our trailer-mounted cafe configuration

A typical outdoor container cafe deployment pattern — the SAMAN shipping container holds the kitchen and counter; a 200–400 sq ft outdoor deck holds the dine-in seating.

Outdoor Seating, Decks, and the Footprint Around Your Container

The container is the building. Most cafe operations need a deck or seating area around it. Budget 200 to 400 sq ft of outdoor space — wooden deck, paved area, or shade structure — alongside the container for cafe tables and chairs. Our deployed outdoor container cafe projects in Bangalore (Koramangala, Whitefield, Yelahanka) and Delhi NCR (Faridabad, Gurgaon, Greater Noida) all use this fixed-container-plus-outdoor-deck pattern. The container holds your kitchen and counter; the deck holds your dine-in.

Frequently Asked Questions

Inside the SAMAN Bangalore factory: every shipping container cafe is new fabrication built to ISO container specification — not a refurbished freight container.

Is this a real used shipping container, or new fabrication built to shipping-container spec?

Every SAMAN shipping container cafe is new fabrication built to ISO shipping-container specification. We do not convert used freight containers because their paint history, floor coatings, and structural condition are not food-safe or consistent unit to unit. The unit you receive has the corrugated steel exterior and ISO dimensions of a shipping container, but the steel is new, the floor is food-grade, and the build quality is the same on every order.

Can a 20-ft shipping container fit a real cafe with seating and kitchen, or do I need a 40-ft?

A 20-ft single (160 sq ft) fits a coffee window or single-product brand with up to 8–10 covers — either stool seating at a counter or a compact dine-in layout. For a back-of-house kitchen zone with separated dine-in seating, you need the 40-ft single or the 25×20 multi-unit. The 40-ft fits 16–20 dine-in covers; the 25×20 multi-unit fits 20–30.

What is the difference between the ₹8,50,000 entry-point and the ₹28,50,000 showcase?

The ₹8,50,000 entry price is a single 20-ft factory-fitted with kitchen position, exhaust provision, electrical, and exterior finish — designed for a coffee window or single-product brand with limited seating. The ₹28,50,000 showcase is two 20-ft containers joined into 500 sq ft, with the same factory-fit standards across both units plus the engineering to join, seal, and align them as one cafe. The price gap covers the second container, the joining work, the larger insulation envelope, and the additional doors, windows, and electrical zones.

Will my shipping container cafe still look like a shipping container after the kitchen is fitted?

Yes — the exterior is unchanged by interior fit-out. Doors, windows, and a service counter are cut into the corrugated wall, but the corten silhouette and ISO container form stay visible. If you want the iconic exposed corrugated steel look, ask for the corten finish. If you prefer a cleaner painted exterior in your brand colour, ask for pre-painted GI cladding. Either way, the shipping-container silhouette remains visible from the street.

Ready to Plan Your Shipping Container Cafe?

Tell us your food concept, your city, and your preferred size — 20-ft, 40-ft, or the 25×20 multi-unit. We come back within 24 hours with a design recommendation, a kitchen layout drawing, and a full quote. No commitment needed at this stage.

📞 +91 97089 89937 · WhatsApp · Get a Quote
short_description:
25×20 ft Multi-Unit Showcase · ISO Shipping-Container Cafe · Factory-Fitted
Shipping container cafe in the iconic ISO form factor. Available as 20-ft single (from ₹8,50,000), 40-ft single, or 25×20 multi-unit showcase. Factory-fitted kitchen, 50mm PUF insulation, FSSAI-ready airflow. Delivered and installed in 15 days from Bangalore and Greater Noida.
Size / Capacity
25×20 ft · 20–30 covers
Frame / Panel
ISO steel · Corten/GI
Price / Delivery
₹28,50,000 · 15 days
Floor Load / Relocations
350 kg/m² · Crane-relocatable
Kitchen Fit
Factory-installed
FSSAI Class
Fixed establishment
Bangalore & Greater Noida factories · Pan-India delivery
Quote in 24 hrs
